about.title: About me
text: I’m Saqib Waheed, a Full Stack Developer specializing in React Native, React.js, Node.js, and MongoDB, with 2.5+ years of hands-on experience building cross-platform mobile and web applications.

My expertise includes React Native CLI, Firebase, WebSockets, REST APIs, HLS video streaming, and cloud-based media workflows using AWS S3 and Wasabi S3. I focus on building scalable, high-performance applications with clean architecture and smooth user experiences.

Currently, I’m working as a React Native Full Stack Developer at Global Seo Creative Technology, where I develop live streaming applications using React Native, React.js, and Node.js. My responsibilities include building REST APIs, implementing WebSocket-based real-time communication systems, integrating HLS video streaming (.m3u8), and developing scalable backend functionalities.

Alongside this role, I’m also working as a Software Engineer at Global Network Creativity Technology, where I work on streaming and AI-powered applications. My work includes integrating HLS video playback, building media upload workflows using AWS S3 and Wasabi S3, developing backend APIs with Node.js and MongoDB, and integrating Gemini API-powered AI features.

Previously, I worked as a React Native Developer at Veeivs, where I developed cross-platform mobile applications for Android and iOS using React Native and integrated REST APIs, Firebase services, and authentication systems.

Before that, I worked as a React Native Developer at X Apparel Private Ltd., where I developed mobile applications with Firebase, Redux, WebSockets, and real-time communication features while contributing to performance optimization and production-level feature integration.

I started my professional journey as a React Native Intern at X Apparel Private Ltd., gaining practical experience in React Native, Expo, Firebase, API integration, debugging, and responsive mobile UI development.
I hold a Bachelor of Science in Software Engineering from the National University of Modern Languages, Islamabad (2021–2024).
